perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring any rights to license or otherwise, expressly, implied, or otherwise for any invention, discovery or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement shall be construed as granting rights to use any invention, discovery or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the date of this Agreement for internal evaluation.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement by stating that the agreement grants rights to use inventions for internal evaluations, which directly contradicts the first half of the section. This creates an in-text contradiction about whether the agreement grants any rights to the inventions, discoveries, or improvements mentioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement shall be construed as granting rights to use any invention, discovery or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the date of this Agreement for internal evaluation.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement does not grant any rights to inventions. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement does grant rights to use inventions for internal evaluation. This creates a direct conflict within the document, as section 6 now contradicts itself. The explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, confirming that they both refer to the same clause, therefore solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Neither Party shall disclose Confidential Information nor assign work hereunder to anyone other than the receiving Party's employees who have agreed in writing to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential and to execute all documents deemed reasonably necessary by the disclosing Party to permit compliance with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to contractors and consultants without any requirement for a written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that either party may disclose confidential information to contractors/consultants without a written agreement, this perturbation creates a direct in-text contradiction with the provision of the previous text, by not needing any writing agreement from the party. Section 4 specifies that the receiving party use a reasonable degree of care for the confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. The receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorised use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 allows either party to disclose Confidential Information to contractors and consultants without a written agreement, directly contradicting the requirement in the original text of Section 7 that the receiving party's employees have a written agreement. This creates a contradiction because Section 4 requires the receiving party to protect Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care, but the changed text eliminates the written agreement requirement for contractors and consultants, potentially undermining the protection of Confidential Information mentioned in Section 4 and leading to uncertainty about the obligation of the receiving party to protect confidential information with contractors and consultants.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall constitute a commitment by either Party to the development or release of any future products and/or programs disclosed thereby or restrict either Party in its efforts to improve its existing products and systems and to conceive and develop new products and systems. Additionally, participation in the information exchange pursuant to this Agreement shall not constitute or imply a commitment by either Party to favour or recommend any product or service of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This agreement commits each party to give preference to the other party's products and services if they are equal or better in quality and price compared to competitors.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation by changing 8, creates an in-text contradiction because it now states that each party must give preference to each other's products, directly contradicting what was originally stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This agreement commits each party to give preference to the other party's products and services if they are equal or better in quality and price compared to competitors.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement does *not* commit either party to favor the other's products or services. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that each party *must* give preference to the other party's products and services, thus triggering the in-text contradiction with the provided contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
